summary: The implementation covers most core requirements including user authentication, wallet system, tournament joining, and admin panel. However, several specific features from the requirements are missing or incomplete, particularly around user registration flow, transaction history display, and certain admin functionalities. Some hallucinated features include reject deposit functionality and match-related data structures that were not requested.

missing_requirements.requirement: User registration with email and password
missing_requirements.reason: The implementation uses Internet Identity authentication instead of traditional email/password registration. There is no registration form or password storage mechanism visible in the code.

missing_requirements.requirement: Users can log in with registered credentials
missing_requirements.reason: Login is handled through Internet Identity provider, not email/password credentials as specified in requirements.

missing_requirements.requirement: Display transaction history on Wallet/Deposit page
missing_requirements.reason: According to REQ-7 acceptance criteria, the Wallet/Deposit page should show transaction history, but no implementation of transaction history display is visible in the diff.
While admin can set match time, there is no evidence of customizable match schedule functionality beyond a single timestamp.
The LoginRegister page is mentioned in routing but its implementation is not shown in the diff summary, so cannot verify if it contains proper authentication forms.

hallucinated_features.reason: Admin can reject deposits, but requirements only specify approval functionality. REQ-3 states admin can approve deposits with no mention of rejection capability.
Backend includes MatchInfo type, matches map, and match-related functions, but requirements specify that match result tracking and scoring are non-goals.

hallucinated_features.description: User search functionality in admin panel
hallucinated_features.reason: Both UserManagement and JoinRequestsList components include search filters, which were not specified in any requirement.
